Mr. Peabody & Sherman (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $111,506,430Details
International Box Office $158,300,000
Worldwide Box Office $269,806,430
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $19,933,402
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $10,907,617
Total $30,841,019
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Mr. Peabody, the most accomplished dog in the world, and his mischievous boy Sherman, use their time machine - The WABAC - to go on the most outrageous adventures known to man or dog. But when Sherman takes The WABAC out for a joyride to impress his friend Penny, they accidentally rip a hole in the universe, wreaking havoc on the most important events in world history. Before they forever alter the past, present and future, Mr. Peabody must come to their rescue, ultimately facing the most daunting challenge of any era: figuring out how to be a parent. Together, the time traveling trio will make their mark on history.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$32,207,057 (28.9% of total gross)
Legs:3.46 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:41.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$145,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,934 opening theaters/3,951 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $163,233,405

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 7th, 2014 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: February 20th, 2014 (Wide) (Argentina)
February 27th, 2014 (Wide) (Germany)
February 28th, 2014 (Wide) (Brazil)
March 6th, 2014 (Wide) (Bahrain)
March 7th, 2014 (Wide) (Spain)
Video Release: October 14th, 2014 by Fox Home Entertainment
October 6th, 2014 by Dreamworks Animation
MPA Rating: PG for some mild action and brief rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2305, 1/15/2014)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Breaking the Fourth Wall, Epilogue, Intertitle, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Plot point: Coming of Age, First Love, Letting Go, Non-Chronological, Romance, Single Parent, Social Worker, Talking Animals, Time Travel
Social setting: Adopted Family
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Bullwinkle Studios, DreamWorks Animation, PDI
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Mr. Peabody, the most accomplished dog in the world, and his mischievous boy Sherman, use their time machine - The WABAC - to go on the most outrageous adventures known to man or dog. But when Sherman takes The WABAC out for a joyride to impress his friend Penny, they accidentally rip a hole in the universe, wreaking havoc on the most important events in world history. Before they forever alter the past, present and future, Mr. Peabody must come to their rescue, ultimately facing the most daunting challenge of any era: figuring out how to be a parent. Together, the time traveling trio will make their mark on history.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$32,207,057 (28.9% of total gross)
Legs:3.46 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:41.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$145,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,934 opening theaters/3,951 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $163,233,405
